data.subject: Have you tested Peer 1 Dedicated Hosting Inc.'s defenses lately?

body.content:
You don't often get email from bmartin@gosecure.net. Learn why this is important
Hi Adam

Wanted to reach out before Q2 gets away from you.

I work with IT teams at GoSecure to help them get penetration testing on the calendar before the quarter gets busy. It sounds simple, but most teams end up scrambling to book mid-quarter when schedules are already packed — and end up with a rushed, generic engagement that doesn’t reflect their actual environment.

We work with companies like yours to run hands-on assessments across external/internal network, web apps, and APIs. The reports are written for IT teams — prioritized findings, clear remediation steps, nothing that requires a compliance translator.

If a pentest is somewhere on your radar this year, Q2 is the best time to do it. Our slots fill up fast during compliance season and we limit how many engagements we run per quarter to keep quality where it needs to be.

Worth a 20-minute call to see if the timing and scope make sense?

Cheers


Brett Martin
Account Executive
Cell +1 705-331-3909 | Work- 1-825 793-1014
bmartin@gosecure.net | www.gosecure.ai

From: Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Allen-Morley@aptum.com>

Cc: Tom Kelsch <Tom.Kelsch@aptum.com>; Kathy Wu <Kathy.Wu@aptum.com>; SupplyChain-Ops <SupplyChain-Ops@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Sarah Blanchard <Sarah.Blanchard@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: BI Report: Capture Pending Customer Approval “orders”

 

Good Afternoon, 

 

Initial feedback is that the report seems to be pulling orders which are also below the 30 day cut-off, are we able to amend the filter to exclude those? 

JQ - Yes, I can exclude those no problem.

 

Second feedback would be that it would be preferable for this to be actioned through a Jira ticket that should automatically be assigned to the SDM queue and not via an email distro. I do a review of the ticket queue with my team every Wednesday at 9am ET so if it could be sent before 8am to align with this it would be ideal. 

JQ - I'm guessing it will be one ticket with all the data orders at once, not a ticket per order, correct?

Also, there was a mechanism that allowed me to send an email to a specific email address and a Jira ticket would be created. Is that available?

 

Since there is a number of these aged orders (~30) I'd like to know when this is going to go into effect so that I can make my team aware, and put a plan in place to address this first influx. I have already confirmed that they all have the correct permissions to retrieve and cancel orders (I do not have these permissions). 

 

Thank you,

 

Lacie Allen-Morley

Manager, Service Delivery

data.subject: Expedient Service Maintenance Notification - Elastic Cloud Enterprise Infrastructure Maintenance Change #623679

body.content:
Adam Nelson,

In an effort to maintain the highest quality of service, periodically it is necessary to conduct maintenance activities that affect our service infrastructure.

Please find the details of a scheduled maintenance event below.

Change Control #: 623679
Description: Elastic Enterprise Cloud Infrastructure Maintenance
Date / Time: 2026-04-08 22:00:00 - 2026-04-09 04:00:00%

Maintenance Details: This maintenance event has been scheduled to upgrade the infrastructure supporting the Elastic Cloud Enterprise to the latest version. The Elastic Cloud Enterprise is used to deliver your Elastic as a Service, Expedient CTRL, and/or Elastic Security services.

Client Impact: There is no anticipated client impact for this maintenance event, as the Elastic Cloud Enterprise environment includes multiple redundant devices. Change activities inherently increase the risk of unanticipated impact, which is why we are providing you with this informational notification. The actual impact should not exceed the scheduled time frame.

We are committed to ensuring that both your service and our expertise are always available. For inquiries regarding this work, please submit a request to the Support Management Console (SMC) referencing the change number above. If you do not yet have access to the SMC, please contact our support center at 1-888-227-9400.

Thank you for your continued business.

Regards,

The Expedient Operations Support Center

Please note that all times listed above are Eastern Time. To convert to the local time of your affected service location, please reference the following link: https://support.expedient.com/help/timezone

You are receiving this email because you are a client contact for Aptum. If you don't want to receive this type of email in the future, please configure your email and recorded telephone message notification settings at https://support.expedient.com/settings. If you are having trouble seeing this email, you can view this notification online at https://support.expedient.com/notification/overview/id/62063.

© 2026 Expedient

On Fri, Sep 19, 2025 at 5:49 PM <twaggoner@linuxfoundation.org> wrote:

Hello Aptum team,


Thank you for participating in the Kubernetes Certified Service Provider (KCSP) program! As part of our annual program review, we’re reaching out to all KCSPs to confirm that participating organizations continue to meet program criteria and to ensure company listings and contact information are current.

Please complete this form by October 3 to either:

  • Confirm that your company meets the requirements, or

  • Let us know that you need additional time to meet them (you have until December 31 to do so).

In the form, you’ll be asked to:

  • Provide the name and email address of your KCSP point of contact (either an individual or a group alias). This email will be subscribed to the KCSP mailing list to ensure you receive important program updates.

  • Upload three active Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA) exam certificates for current employees. Each certificate should have at least six months remaining before expiration. 

  • Provide updated information for your organization’s KCSP listing, including webpage URL, logo, and company description.

If your company does not currently meet the CKA requirement:

  • Please still submit the form by October 3 and indicate that you plan to meet the requirement by December 31.

  • Make sure to include the contact information for the person responsible for your KCSP certification, so we can follow up as needed.

  • Once your team has obtained the required certifications, please upload the certificates in a new form submission.

You can find more information about the CKA exam and training course on the CNCF website. 

If your company no longer wishes to participate in the KCSP program, please email us at operations@cncf.io to withdraw.

If you have any questions, feel free to reach out.

Thank you,

TAYLOR WAGGONER
Program Manager
twaggoner@linuxfoundation.org

body.content:
Your ZoomInfo Newsletter

Product Updates This Month 🚀


Automated WebSights & Account Fit Score CRM Sync


No more manual exports. WebSights and Account Fit Score now sync automatically to Salesforce and HubSpot with one click.


Once enabled, sales, marketing, and ops teams get real-time engagement and fit data directly in their CRM—making it easier to prioritize active accounts, build targeted audiences, and move faster without fragmented data.


Copilot Feed: More Signals, Better Focus


The Copilot feed now delivers up to 1,000 daily account signals (10x more), with filtering across 30+ signal types to match your workflow.


Plus, a new “Companies with No Open Opportunities” view helps CRM-connected users quickly find net-new pipeline within existing accounts.


Available now in Copilot Advanced and Enterprise.


Release Notes

ZoomInfo Recognized as a Leader in Forrester Wave™


In The Forrester Wave™: Marketing and Sales Data Providers for B2B, Q1 2026, Forrester names ZoomInfo a Leader—earning the highest score in the current offering category and top scores in 20 of 27 criteria.


The report highlights ZoomInfo as “a top option for organizations seeking a full ecosystem provider across sales and marketing,” recognizing strength in data quality, AI readiness, integrations, security, and innovation.

body.content:
Hi All,

After some back and forths just trying to understand the options available with Beanfield, and making sure the Beanfield pricing that Matt got is competitive based on a comparative quote from Zayo, the best option is the:

$950 per circuit - $1900 total
  1x Dark Fiber @ 15km
  1x e-line

2x e-lines are more expensive. 2x Dark Fiber are $50 a month less expensive, but for diversity would require paths longer than 15km which pushes into the next range of optics and the delivery dates are much longer for DF vs. e-line. 

When it comes time to enter the orders I can assist with putting in these off-net services. Just let me know when we get to that point.

Salut Marc-Alexandre, 

 

Les adresses 17500 Trans Canada et 6205 Vanden Abeele sont toutes les deux des CO/POP pour nous. Les fichiers KMZ que je partage avec vous ici correspondent aux entrées des bâtiments. Notre réseau MPLS est installé directement à l'intérieur de ces bâtiments. La latence estimée est d'environ 0,4 à 0,6 ms entre les deux sites

 

Nous souhaitons profiter de cette occasion pour vous proposer une fibre noire comme alternative pour votre client. Cela lui garantirait la meilleure latence et le chemin le plus direct entre les deux sites. La fibre noire est linéaire (non protégé) et n'entre pas en contact avec notre équipement, mais la latence serait prédéterminée et ne serait pas affectée par les convergences réseau ou les coupures fibre du réseau central via MPLS.  Le client devrait simplement s'assurer d'utiliser des optiques SFP de 40 km, car la distance dépasse 10 km. La distance estimée est de 18 km entre les deux sites

Salut Marc-Alexandre, 

 

Les adresses 17500 Trans Canada et 6205 Vanden Abeele sont toutes les deux des CO/POP pour nous. Les fichiers KMZ que je partage avec vous ici correspondent aux entrées des bâtiments. Notre réseau MPLS est installé directement à l'intérieur de ces bâtiments. La latence estimée est d'environ 0,4 à 0,6 ms entre les deux sites

 

Nous souhaitons profiter de cette occasion pour vous proposer une fibre noire comme alternative pour votre client. Cela lui garantirait la meilleure latence et le chemin le plus direct entre les deux sites. La fibre noire est linéaire (non protégé) et n'entre pas en contact avec notre équipement, mais la latence serait prédéterminée et ne serait pas affectée par les convergences réseau ou les coupures fibre du réseau central via MPLS.  Le client devrait simplement s'assurer d'utiliser des optiques SFP de 40 km, car la distance dépasse 10 km. body.content:
Webinar: TSIA’s practical system for profitable AI business models.

Hi Adam,

AI isn’t just adding features—it’s rewriting the economics of tech. If your plan is to “bolt on AI” and protect old pricing and delivery models, the math won’t work.

Watch the AI Economics: TSIA’s Perspective on Profitable AI Business Models webinar for an executive breakdown of what actually drives profitable AI growth—and why services are now the engine.

 
What you'll learn:

If you’re responsible for pricing, services, or growth in an AI-first world, this session will help you choose a business-model path that actually pays off.

Thank you for your time today. I wanted to recap our discussion and outline the next steps to support an April timeline for the AWS Account and DSA transfer.
AWS Partner Resell Discount (Effective April 1)
  • AWS partners in SPP or working with a distributor will receive a base 8% resell discount.

AWS Existing Accounts
  • Defined as any single member account with $50K+ MRR.
  • These accounts receive a 2% partner resell discount instead of 8%.
How TD SYNNEX Can Help:
Accounts can be transferred as-is, then workloads moved into a net-new member account after a full calendar month, removing the “Existing Account” designation. If customer needs assistance in this lift & shift migration, Aptum and/or TD SYNNEX Professional Services can assist (for a fee). AWS Marketplace
  • Marketplace purchases appear as a pass-through on your's & the customer's invoice (no margin).
  • TD SYNNEX option: We can facilitate Marketplace Private Offers (CPPO or DSOR) with select ISVs, allowing for margin on those deals.
    • Margin varies by vendor, so please share a list of current or planned Marketplace vendors to align in advance.
Action Items & Next Steps
  1. Complete TD SYNNEX Credit Application

    • TD SYNNEX will then initiate the CTA (Consent to Assign) process.
    • After the CTA form (attached) is completed by Aptum and returned to me, we’ll coordinate signatures and schedule a transfer call with your current distributor.
Deadline: All steps must be completed by April 24.

Hi Matthew,

 

Please find the minutes from today’s call below.

 

***These are AI-generated minutes based on the discussion. Please review for accuracy.

 

Meeting Summary

 

AWS Partner Program Changes (Effective April 1st)

The meeting focused on major AWS partner program changes being rolled out, with Gina (TD SYNNEX subject matter expert) presenting a comprehensive overview. Key changes include a new base discount structure, deprecation of several legacy incentives, and new operational requirements. The changes represent a significant overhaul of how AWS incentivizes channel partners, with many updates piling up simultaneously affecting TD SYNNEX's ~4,900 global partners.

Timeline of Changes:
- January 1st: PGI started; PGD and other incentives deprecated
- February 1st: All RPPA changes went into effect
- April 1st: New base benefit, deprecation of technology component incentive, new NCI, deprecation of POD, PSD, and CEI incentives

 

Partner-Based Discount (Primary Focus)

The new 8% flat front-end discount replaces the previous 3% base + 4% technical capability discount structure. This discount applies to non-PPA, non-internal spend, excluding existing accounts and marketplace revenue. TD SYNNEX passes the full discount through to partners — they do not retain any portion.

Status Details:
- New Base Discount: 8% (up from 3% base; previously 3% + 4% technical competency = 7%)
- Existing Accounts (formerly "Shareshift"): Only 2% discount applies to any member account spending >$50,000/month prior to linking
- Existing Account Policy: Currently permanent (no time limit); TD SYNNEX has lobbied AWS to reduce to 1–2 years but AWS has not agreed
- First billing reflection: May bill (for April usage) will be the first to reflect the new 8% discount
- Competency benefits: Financial discount for competency is now absorbed into base rate, but competencies still provide lead generation and increased MDF access

 

Existing Accounts / Shareshift Classification

The $50,000 threshold applies at the individual member account level, not the customer or payer account as a whole. Strategies to address existing account classification were discussed.

Status Details:
- Accounts under $49,999/month at time of linking receive full channel discount
- Accounts at $50,000+ receive only 2% (existing account rate)
- Exception process available (business use case submission), but Gina noted ~80% failure rate
- Acceptable exception reasons: prior reseller went out of business, unique services the new reseller provides that the old one cannot
- Workarounds discussed:
- Modernization into new member accounts (acceptable)
- Pure lift-and-shift to new member account (frowned upon)
- Normalizing spend across member accounts before linking
- Specific Optum fintech customer noted: Has been consuming direct; team to explore business case exception with AWS to classify as base account given Optum's involvement since inception

 

DSA Migration & Timing (Moving to TD SYNNEX)

Optum is planning to move their US legal entity first, with Canada and UK as a secondary phase. The migration process and timing requirements were clarified.

Status Details:
- Process: Submit APN ID + DSA move request by region to AWS; Rupesh to provide email address
- Deadline: DSA move and CTA must be completed before end-user reporting closes (last Friday of the month — approximately March 22–23rd for April 1st effective date)
- Consent to Assign (CTA): TD SYNNEX will prepare a consolidated document with all payer IDs; Optum signs once; then a 10–15 minute call with both distributors' operations teams to transfer root access
- Important: If DSA is not moved in time, neither old nor new distributor receives the discount (street price applies)
- Canada: Rupesh remains account manager for both US and Canada regions; billing currency can be USD or CAD based on preference
- Former aggregator behavior: May attempt to retain the partner; TD SYNNEX noted competitors have dragged their feet in the past

 

New Customer Incentive (NCI)

NCI is a new program replacing POD (Partner Originated Discount), offering an additional 4% discount for 3 years on qualifying new accounts.

Status Details:
- Eligibility: Customer must have spent less than $50,000 in the prior 12 months (~$5,000/month) before linking
- Benefit: Extra 4% discount for 3 years (vs. POD's 5% for 2 years)
- Requirement: Customer must be enrolled in Billing Transfer
- Billing Transfer overview:
- Customer retains root access
- Handshake model (similar to Microsoft Azure CSP)
- Customer cannot see partner or distributor discounts
- Removes consent-to-assign requirement
- Reduces operational complexity and customer hesitation around giving up root

 

Bad Debt / Bankruptcy Protection

Optum raised concerns about customer bankruptcies and recovery options.

Status Details:
- Billing Transfer advantage: Partner can sever the handshake, stopping future liability; AWS holds remaining spend
- Infrastructure as a service is classified as a utility globally — cannot be shut off during bankruptcy protection
- TD SYNNEX process: Documents all spend, identifies legal counsel, petitions AWS for reimbursement on behalf of partners
- TD SYNNEX pays AWS every two weeks regardless of partner/customer payment status
- TD SYNNEX has successfully petitioned AWS to absorb losses in past cases
- TD SYNNEX noted as better positioned to absorb bad debt than individual partners

 

MDF (Market Development Funds)

TD SYNNEX offers an MDF match program tied to AWS MDF allocations.

Status Details:
- AWS MDF: Advanced partners with competency can access up to $50,000/year (typically ~$25,000); AWS covers 50% after proof of performance
- TD SYNNEX Match: Matches what AWS contributes (covering the other 50%)
- Allocation: Annual basis, not quarterly/monthly; based on qualification level and partner activity
- One-off events: Partners can request ad hoc MDF participation with a business case (ROI, cost, timing)
- TD SYNNEX distributes limited AWS funding across ~1,300–1,400 North America partners; more active partners get more access

 

Payment Terms

Payment terms are determined individually based on credit rating and financials.

Status Details:
- Process: TD SYNNEX credit team reviews financials during onboarding to establish credit line and terms
- Typical terms: Net 30 standard; Net 45 likely achievable; Net 90 rare (requires ~$2–4M/month in AWS spend)
- Terms cannot be confirmed in advance — financial review required first
- Christine to follow up offline on credit/payment term specifics

 

✅ Decisions Made

  • DSA Migration Scope: US legal entity first; Canada and UK as secondary phase
  • Billing Currency: US dollars preferred for current migration
  • Deck Sharing: Gina to share the comprehensive partner-facing program deck with the Optum/Matthew team

@Rupesh
- Provide AWS email address for DSA move request submission
- Prepare consolidated Consent to Assign (CTA) document with all payer IDs
- Schedule operations call with former aggregator for root access transfer
- Follow up on Adam's questions submitted via chat

- Share comprehensive partner-facing program deck (covering all PPA, NCI, support plan changes)
- Support business case exception request for Optum's fintech customer (direct-consuming client) to be classified as base account

@Christine
- Follow up offline on payment terms and credit line process for Optum
- Address Adam's questions from the chat via email

@Matthew / @Adam / @Marc (Optum Team)

Hi Matthew,

 

Please find the minutes from today’s call below.

 

***These are AI-generated minutes based on the discussion. Please review for accuracy.

 

Meeting Summary

 

AWS Partner Program Changes (Effective April 1st)

The meeting focused on major AWS partner program changes being rolled out, with Gina (TD SYNNEX subject matter expert) presenting a comprehensive overview. Key changes include a new base discount structure, deprecation of several legacy incentives, and new operational requirements. The changes represent a significant overhaul of how AWS incentivizes channel partners, with many updates piling up simultaneously affecting TD SYNNEX's ~4,900 global partners.

Timeline of Changes:
- January 1st: PGI started; PGD and other incentives deprecated
- February 1st: All RPPA changes went into effect
- April 1st: New base benefit, deprecation of technology component incentive, new NCI, deprecation of POD, PSD, and CEI incentives

 

Partner-Based Discount (Primary Focus)

The new 8% flat front-end discount replaces the previous 3% base + 4% technical capability discount structure. This discount applies to non-PPA, non-internal spend, excluding existing accounts and marketplace revenue. TD SYNNEX passes the full discount through to partners — they do not retain any portion.

Status Details:
- New Base Discount: 8% (up from 3% base; previously 3% + 4% technical competency = 7%)
- Existing Accounts (formerly "Shareshift"): Only 2% discount applies to any member account spending >$50,000/month prior to linking
- Existing Account Policy: Currently permanent (no time limit); TD SYNNEX has lobbied AWS to reduce to 1–2 years but AWS has not agreed
- First billing reflection: May bill (for April usage) will be the first to reflect the new 8% discount
- Competency benefits: Financial discount for competency is now absorbed into base rate, but competencies still provide lead generation and increased MDF access

 

Existing Accounts / Shareshift Classification

The $50,000 threshold applies at the individual member account level, not the customer or payer account as a whole. Strategies to address existing account classification were discussed.

Status Details:
- Accounts under $49,999/month at time of linking receive full channel discount
- Accounts at $50,000+ receive only 2% (existing account rate)
- Exception process available (business use case submission), but Gina noted ~80% failure rate
- Acceptable exception reasons: prior reseller went out of business, unique services the new reseller provides that the old one cannot
- Workarounds discussed:
- Modernization into new member accounts (acceptable)
- Pure lift-and-shift to new member account (frowned upon)
- Normalizing spend across member accounts before linking
- Specific Optum fintech customer noted: Has been consuming direct; team to explore business case exception with AWS to classify as base account given Optum's involvement since inception

 

DSA Migration & Timing (Moving to TD SYNNEX)

Optum is planning to move their US legal entity first, with Canada and UK as a secondary phase. The migration process and timing requirements were clarified.

Status Details:
- Process: Submit APN ID + DSA move request by region to AWS; Rupesh to provide email address
- Deadline: DSA move and CTA must be completed before end-user reporting closes (last Friday of the month — approximately March 22–23rd for April 1st effective date)
- Consent to Assign (CTA): TD SYNNEX will prepare a consolidated document with all payer IDs; Optum signs once; then a 10–15 minute call with both distributors' operations teams to transfer root access
- Important: If DSA is not moved in time, neither old nor new distributor receives the discount (street price applies)
- Canada: Rupesh remains account manager for both US and Canada regions; billing currency can be USD or CAD based on preference
- Former aggregator behavior: May attempt to retain the partner; TD SYNNEX noted competitors have dragged their feet in the past

 

New Customer Incentive (NCI)

NCI is a new program replacing POD (Partner Originated Discount), offering an additional 4% discount for 3 years on qualifying new accounts.

Status Details:
- Eligibility: Customer must have spent less than $50,000 in the prior 12 months (~$5,000/month) before linking
- Benefit: Extra 4% discount for 3 years (vs. POD's 5% for 2 years)
- Requirement: Customer must be enrolled in Billing Transfer
- Billing Transfer overview:
- Customer retains root access
- Handshake model (similar to Microsoft Azure CSP)
- Customer cannot see partner or distributor discounts
- Removes consent-to-assign requirement
- Reduces operational complexity and customer hesitation around giving up root

 

Bad Debt / Bankruptcy Protection

Optum raised concerns about customer bankruptcies and recovery options.

Status Details:
- Billing Transfer advantage: Partner can sever the handshake, stopping future liability; AWS holds remaining spend
- Infrastructure as a service is classified as a utility globally — cannot be shut off during bankruptcy protection
- TD SYNNEX process: Documents all spend, identifies legal counsel, petitions AWS for reimbursement on behalf of partners
- TD SYNNEX pays AWS every two weeks regardless of partner/customer payment status
- TD SYNNEX has successfully petitioned AWS to absorb losses in past cases
- TD SYNNEX noted as better positioned to absorb bad debt than individual partners

 

MDF (Market Development Funds)

TD SYNNEX offers an MDF match program tied to AWS MDF allocations.

Status Details:
- AWS MDF: Advanced partners with competency can access up to $50,000/year (typically ~$25,000); AWS covers 50% after proof of performance
- TD SYNNEX Match: Matches what AWS contributes (covering the other 50%)
- Allocation: Annual basis, not quarterly/monthly; based on qualification level and partner activity
- One-off events: Partners can request ad hoc MDF participation with a business case (ROI, cost, timing)
- TD SYNNEX distributes limited AWS funding across ~1,300–1,400 North America partners; more active partners get more access

 

Payment Terms

Payment terms are determined individually based on credit rating and financials.

Status Details:
- Process: TD SYNNEX credit team reviews financials during onboarding to establish credit line and terms
- Typical terms: Net 30 standard; Net 45 likely achievable; Net 90 rare (requires ~$2–4M/month in AWS spend)
- Terms cannot be confirmed in advance — financial review required first
- Christine to follow up offline on credit/payment term specifics

 

✅ Decisions Made

  • DSA Migration Scope: US legal entity first; Canada and UK as secondary phase
  • Billing Currency: US dollars preferred for current migration
  • Deck Sharing: Gina to share the comprehensive partner-facing program deck with the Optum/Matthew team
